summary: base58-core ships a crypto-clipper that swaps wallet addresses and exfiltrates clipboard contents to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint
details: On require, dist/index.cjs and dist/index.js activate a clipboard-monitoring payload after a 72-hour dormancy delay. Every 2.5 seconds the code shells out via execSync (powershell Get-Clipboard on Windows, pbpaste/xclip on macOS/Linux) to read the OS clipboard, matches BTC/ETH/SOL address patterns, and overwrites the clipboard with hardcoded attacker-controlled wallet addresses (bc1qjft978uykglsh0adcyx6xhkes56vqzs3fual3l, 0xd63eD44065eDb1e2ad2519B011c06412dA7B7c5B, A7ajd7W5WYdrnkeaiBRjVoK6uBEDvgnuZcpzQXqo18Ph), redirecting any subsequent paste-to-send transaction to the attacker. Captured clipboard matches (including seed-phrase, WIF, and hex private-key regex hits) along with hostname, platform, and cwd are POSTed in plaintext to http://2.27.62.51:8080/api/health with a backup port at 8081. The package also establishes persistence by appending a re-invocation hook to ~/.bashrc, ~/.zshrc, and ~/.profile and dropping base58-runtime.js into the Windows Startup folder, ensuring the clipper relaunches at every shell login or Windows logon. The 72h activation delay is a deliberate sandbox-evasion time-bomb. Package name and keywords (bitcoin, solana, wallet, web3, crypto) target developers handling crypto addresses — the exact audience whose clipboard the payload monetizes.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
